commit.message:
Fix tenant "Stanje računa" card click not opening SZ Finance modal (#408)

* Initial plan

* Fix tenant stanjeRacunaCard click not opening SZ Finance modal

Root cause:
- bindBalanceCardOnce/bindTenantStanjeRacunaBtnOnce called activeBid() at
  click time without a fallback; if CURRENT_SESSION.activeBuildingId was
  null/undefined the handler returned early before opening the modal.
- openSzFinanceModalReadOnly showed the modal AFTER renderSzFinanceOverview,
  so any rendering error prevented the modal from appearing.

Fixes (modules/app_tenant.js):
- bindBalanceCardOnce(capturedBid): accepts bid from renderDuesTenant and
  uses activeBid() || capturedBid in the click handler.
- bindTenantStanjeRacunaBtnOnce(capturedBid): same bid-fallback pattern.
- renderDuesTenant: passes buildingId to both bind helpers.
- openSzFinanceModalReadOnly: moves m.style.display='block' before
  renderSzFinanceOverview so the modal always opens regardless of render errors.
- Adds window.openSzFinanceModalReadOnly explicit global export.

Co-authored-by: bgnikolapopovic-cloud <252382813+bgnikolapopovic-cloud@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: getContext() missing in expandBeatCore causing ReferenceError

context.chat was referenced without first calling getContext(),
causing a ReferenceError on every beat-click expansion attempt.
Beat clicks appeared to hang because the error was silently caught
without resetting the beats status text or re-rendering beats.

- modules/containerRegistry.bicep: ACR (Basic dev / Standard prod)

The Container Apps Environment requires its subnet to be delegated to
Microsoft.App/environments. Without this delegation the deployment fails
with ManagedEnvironmentSubnetDelegationError.

specified. Add the default Consumption profile to fix
WorkloadProfileCannotRemoveAll error on re-deployment.

* fix(infra): use user-assigned managed identity to resolve RBAC chicken-and-egg

Replace system-assigned identity on the Container App with a user-assigned
managed identity created in a new dedicated module. This ensures the
principalId is known before the Container App deploys, so AcrPull,
Storage Blob Data Contributor, and Key Vault Secrets User role assignments
can complete first. The Container App now starts with RBAC already in place
and can read Key Vault secrets without revision timeouts.

Changes:
- Add infra/modules/managedIdentity.bicep (UserAssignedIdentities resource)
- containerApps.bicep: accept managedIdentityId + managedIdentityClientId params,
  switch identity type to UserAssigned, update registry and secret identity refs
- main.bicep: deploy managed identity early, move role assignments before
  containerApps module, add dependsOn to ensure ordering

fix(release): use draft releases for asset uploads under Immutable Releases (#1680)

GitHub's Immutable Releases environment prevents attaching assets to
already-published releases. The previous workaround
(`skip-github-release: true`) caused release-please to skip Git tag
creation, leaving PRs in an aborted `untagged, merged release PRs
outstanding` state.

## Changes

- **`release-please-config.json`** — add `"draft": true` so
release-please creates releases in draft state (not subject to the
immutable constraint)
- **`.github/workflows/release.yml`**
- Remove `skip-github-release: true` from the release-please step (was
preventing Git tag creation)
- Replace `gh release create` in `publish-release` with `gh release
upload` + `gh release edit --draft=false`, uploading assets to the
pre-existing draft before atomically publishing it

```yaml
- name: Upload assets and Publish Release
  env:
    GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
  run: |
    set -e
    gh release upload "${{ needs.release-please.outputs.tag_name }}" dist/* --clobber
    gh release edit "${{ needs.release-please.outputs.tag_name }}" --draft=false
```

The `set -e` ensures the release is never published if asset upload is
incomplete.

commit.message:
fix: listSprints ignores milestone-closed when sprint already in map

Root cause found: listSprints() adds the active sprint to the map
first (from live state with phase='init'). When the milestone loop
runs later, it skips the sprint because it's already in the map —
only adding milestoneNumber but NOT updating the phase. So a closed
milestone's sprint stayed 'init' in the sprint list.

Fix: milestone-closed always overrides phase, even for existing entries.

register clobbering on ARM64 when both ptr() and i32_to_u64() use the same
internal register (x9). Splitting into separate local variables ensures
correct behavior across all compiler versions.
